de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy.breakpoint

Interface IBreakpoint

  • All Known Implementing Classes:
    AbstractBreakpoint, AbstractConditionalBreakpoint, AbstractHitCountBreakpoint, ExceptionBreakpoint, FieldWatchpoint, KeYWatchpoint, LineBreakpoint, MethodBreakpoint, SymbolicExecutionExceptionBreakpoint

    public interface IBreakpoint
    Defines the basic functionality of a breakpoint.
    Author:
    Martin Hentschel

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean isBreakpointHit(SourceElement activeStatement, RuleApp ruleApp, Proof proof, Node node)
      Determines if the breakpoint represented by this BreakpointStopConition is triggered.
      boolean isEnabled()
      Checks if the Breakpoint is enabled.
      void updateState(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal)
      This method is called from ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition.isGoalAllowed(int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) and can be used to update the state of the IBreakpoint.

    • Method Detail

      • isEnabled

        boolean isEnabled()
        Checks if the Breakpoint is enabled.
        Returns:
        true if Breakpoint is enabled

      • updateState

        void updateState(int maxApplications,
               long timeout,
               Proof proof,
               IGoalChooser goalChooser,
               long startTime,
               int countApplied,
               Goal goal)
        This method is called from ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition.isGoalAllowed(int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) and can be used to update the state of the IBreakpoint.
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.

      • isBreakpointHit

        boolean isBreakpointHit(SourceElement activeStatement,
                      RuleApp ruleApp,
                      Proof proof,
                      Node node)
        Determines if the breakpoint represented by this BreakpointStopConition is triggered. Override this method in order to suspend execution when a breakpoint is hit.
        Parameters:
        activeStatement - the activeStatement of the node
        ruleApp - the applied RuleApp
        proof - the current proof
        node - the current node
        Returns:
        true if execution should hold
